title: Lean local Unity development bootstrap
objective: Establish a practical A1 local-development lane for Sasha using the installed Unity 6000.5.4f1 Editor and creator-approved direct MCP route, then create the Unity project skeleton, deterministic code seams, tests, and a non-gameplay technical interaction sandbox without production release authority.
value: Starts building the game while retaining Git checkpoints, protected review, explicit Unity targeting, creator-controlled credentials, and reversible local iteration.
in_scope: Activate repository/bootstrap work before Game exists; the creator may then create and open Game using Unity 6000.5.4f1 and record actual package versions.
Use only the creator-approved UNITY-MCP-EXTERNAL route for agent-originated Unity interaction; the creator opens the Editor and approves the exact Game project connection.

Create the smallest coherent project skeleton, deterministic SimulationCore and non-persisting SaveContracts interfaces, edit/play-mode tests, one non-gameplay technical scene, and basic camera/debug UI. This packet writes no save bytes or frozen save schema.

Use ordinary non-main Git branches, pre-change checkpoints, protected pull requests, and required checks. The initial project/CI merge is creator-controlled; later auto-merge requires a new WP-0003 CI required check.
Keep Unity caches and user-local generated state ignored while committing inspectable source, project settings, manifests, and tests.
Capture screenshots, test results, changed paths, and known limits under BuildArtifacts/WP-0003 and docs/evidence/WP-0003.
non_goals: Production-engine ratification, native release acceptance, distribution, publishing, monetization, or store integration.
Unity account, organization, seat, license, billing, purchase, or cloud-service changes.
Direct agent or CI invocation of Unity Hub, Editor, Unity CLI, executable, or batchmode.
Unapproved package, SDK, dependency, external-tool, or service installation.
Production art, final narrative, final economy balance, final save migration, or irreversible content commitments.
City grammar, colony simulation, vehicle embodiment, faction behavior, economy, or other creator-owned gameplay decisions.
Constitutional, governance, credential, dependency, save-migration, release, or publishing auto-merge.
interfaces: Codex -> conditional first-use gate -> creator-approved Unity MCP Bridge -> exact Game project
Git checkpoint -> bounded local edit -> tests/captures -> protected pull request
Plain C# deterministic state -> thin Unity presentation adapter
Non-persisting SaveContracts interfaces -> later separately reviewed save implementation

save_impact.summary: S0 for activation: no existing player saves and no migration. Any save-format freeze or migration requires a later reviewed change.
save_impact.compatibility: Development saves are disposable until a later packet explicitly freezes a versioned contract.

acceptance_tests.oracle: A creator-protected activation receipt binds the compact local boundary manifest, exact packet contract, clean non-main Git checkpoint, intended Game target, Unity 6000.5.4f1 development authority, conditional first-use MCP mode, and allowed/denied action sets.
Unity Bridge is running, Codex is selected and approved, and the connection targets exactly the repository Game project before any Unity tool call.
Foundation validation passes without weakening protected history or repository enforcement.
The candidate contains no whitespace errors and remains within declared paths.
The creator-opened Game project imports and compiles without Unity console errors.
The WP-0003 EditMode test set passes through the creator-approved Unity MCP route.
The non-gameplay technical interaction sandbox enters Play Mode, responds to its bounded input, and exits cleanly.
Two identical fixed-tick technical input sequences produce identical canonical core-state hashes.
One screenshot records the compiling technical scene and debug state without claiming production art or gameplay acceptance.

triggers: The MCP target differs from Game.
A requested action crosses the denied-action boundary.
Required checks fail or protected repository settings drift.
Unity, package, save, or project changes become unrecoverable by ordinary Git reversal.
steps: Stop Unity MCP work and preserve logs and screenshots.
Revert the bounded branch or delete it without rewriting main history.
Restore the last clean Git checkpoint and reopen only after creator review.
rollback.save_recovery: Development saves are disposable. Preserve any useful failing fixture separately before deleting the development profile.

known_limits: The retained manual log is an evidence and attempt narrative, not a raw MCP transport transcript.
Unity compile, EditMode, PlayMode, project-target, and capture results are Codex-produced implementation evidence; GitHub Actions did not execute those manual gates.
